{{Quote|We'll be disassembled, cannibalized for scrap, sent to the sandmines of Neelgaimon or the orbital factories around Ryloon!|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]|<ref name="Children of the Jedi (novel)">[[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']]</ref>}}
'''Neelgaimon''' was a [[Temperature/Legends|hot]], drought-ridden [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Xappyh sector/Legends|Xappyh sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].

==History==
The planet was famous for its hazardous sandmines, the planet's main industry, which were developed and maintained by [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] and [[Droid/Legends|droid]] labor. It was still an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] world by [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]].

[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] visited Neelgaimon once.

==Behind the Scenes==
The planet appears to be a [[Tuckerization]] of the name of the British writer [[wikipedia:Neil Gaiman|Neil Gaiman]]. [[Barbara Hambly]], author of ''[[Children of the Jedi]]'', also included another Tuckerization of Neil Gaiman's name in the character [[Gayman Neeloid]], in her second ''Star Wars'' novel, ''[[Planet of Twilight]]''.

{{Planet-stub}}
